text.title: pain-point-research Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for pain-point-research as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Surface-level research: reading only post titles, not comments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Titles are clickbait; the pain signal is in the comments where users explain their situation with specificity.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-source research is blind to the pain landscape; each tier reveals a different aspect of the problem space.

failure-mode.description: Not quantifying frequency or severity — treating all pains as equally important.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without scoring, the loudest pain (most vocal users) wins over the most frequent pain (most users affected).

failure-mode.description: Ignoring who is complaining; noting only what they complain about.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Segment matters: a pain that is severe for freelancers may be irrelevant for enterprise users even if the surface complaint is identical.

failure-mode.description: Confirmation bias: only documenting expected pain points.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unexpected pains are often the most actionable; filtering them out produces a research report that confirms assumptions rather than challenges them.
